generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor capturing occupancy status within building spaces, integrated into BIM model with metadata annotations for MQTT communication via Eclipse Hono/Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "BAS sensor capturing environmental conditions (e.g., temperature, humidity, daylight levels) with real-time data transmission via Eclipse Hono's lightweight MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor measuring real-time energy consumption of building systems, with metadata extraction for Ditto-based twin management."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured operational data representing occupancy status transmitted via Eclipse Hono's MQTT-based messaging to Eclipse Ditto for digital twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental data (e.g., temperature, humidity) transmitted via Eclipse Hono's lightweight protocol to Ditto for semantic consistency with BIM schemas."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance EnergyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured energy consumption data transmitted via MQTT through Eclipse Hono, normalized to BIM-based schemas for unified interpretation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimStaticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich 3D BIM model representing building geometry, topology, and component relationships, enriched with device metadata for sensors/actuators."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoDittoConfigurator, UnrealEngine5
]

instance RuntimeDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured real-time operational data (e.g., sensor readings, control signals) managed by Eclipse Ditto as digital twin instances for each physical device."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted, EnergyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoTwinManager
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Domain-specific control semantics (e.g., lighting strategies, HVAC setpoints) embedded within BIM via TwinLight's layered architecture."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TwinLightFramework
]

instance VisualizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization model rendered in Unreal Engine 5 for immersive monitoring and simulation of building operations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngine5
]

instance AnalyticsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning models (e.g., reinforcement learning in BOREALIS) for predictive optimization and anomaly detection, integrated as custom DT services."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BorealisRLLibrary
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelDrivenEngineering : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automates extraction of domain semantics from BIM models via model-to-model/text transformations, generating runtime artifacts (e.g., device configurations)."
    DTDFVocab:enables BimStaticModelGenerationService, RuntimeArtifactGenerationService
]

instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight MQTT-based messaging protocol enabling real-time bidirectional data exchange between physical sensors/actuators and digital twins."
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataTransmissionService, ActuatorCommandRoutingService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Manages digital twin instances, metadata, and bidirectional communication with physical devices, supporting scalable twin management."
    DTDFVocab:enables TwinManagementService, MetadataSynchronizationService
]

instance UnrealEngine5 : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables rich, interactive 3D visualization for monitoring and simulation with plugin support for custom DT services."
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveMonitoringService, SimulationServices
]

instance BimTools : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Used by Building Control Engineers to model devices and define control logic, serving as the semantic backbone for the digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ControlLogicDefinitionService
]

instance BorealisRLLibrary : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Reinforcement learning libraries integrated for adaptive control policies evolving based on real-time sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveControlPoliciesService, DynamicOptimizationService
]

instance HonoDittoConfigurator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scans BIM models for 'twinnable' devices, extracts metadata, and establishes MQTT-based communication channels for real-time data streaming."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceTwinningService, CommunicationChannelSetupService
]

instance TwinLightFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates bidirectional communication between digital twins and physical lighting control systems with layered architecture for control logic."

]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous visualization of building performance metrics (e.g., energy consumption, occupancy) via Unreal Engine 5's immersive 3D interface."

]

]

]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The virtual-to-physical interaction in this DT system involves **automated control commands** generated by the DT and transmitted back to the physical building. This is enabled through **bidirectional communication** between the virtual and physical systems, as demonstrated in the **TwinLight project**, which embeds control logic (e.g., lighting dimming commands) directly into BIM models. The **BOREALIS project** further extends this by using **reinforcement learning** to iteratively improve control policies, ensuring adaptive and optimized building operations. While specific control signals like HVAC setpoints are not explicitly mentioned, the framework supports **runtime configuration management** and **automated commissioning** to enable dynamic adjustments."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale for this DT system is characterized by **real-time synchronization** between the physical building and its digital twin. The framework supports **live data exchange** and **sub-second updates** to ensure continuous alignment between the virtual and physical systems. While specific latency metrics (e.g., <1 second) are not explicitly detailed, the use of **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for real-time connectivity implies low-latency interactions. The system is designed to accommodate both **real-time updates** (e.g., sensor data) and **batch processing** for historical analysis, though the document does not specify periodic updates (e.g., weekly or monthly)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **DT constellation** is orchestrated as a **modular, service-oriented architecture** with the following components:
  1. **Core Framework**: A **centralized DT platform** built on **BIM + Hono/Ditto**, responsible for **data ingestion, synchronization, and basic control**.
  2. **Middleware Layer**: **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** handle **real-time data routing**, **device twin management**, and **bidirectional communication**.
  3. **Visualization Layer**: **Unreal Engine 5** provides an **immersive interface** for **Facility Managers** to interact with the DT.
  4. **Service Layer**: **Custom plugins** (e.g., analytics, AI models) extend the DT’s functionality via **standardized extension points**, enabling **scalable integration** of third-party services.
  5. **Control Layer**: **Building Control Engineers** define **device configurations and control logic** within the BIM model, which are then **automatically deployed** to the physical systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates across the following **life cycle stages** of the physical building:
  1. **Design Phase**: The DT is initialized from **BIM models** created during the **design and construction phases**, incorporating **static asset data** (e.g., geometry, spatial relationships).
  2. **Commissioning Phase**: The **TwinLight** framework automates the **initial deployment** of the DT by extracting **device metadata** from BIM and configuring **runtime interfaces** for bidirectional communication.
  3. **Operational Phase**: The DT enables **real-time monitoring, control, and optimization** during the building’s **operation and maintenance** lifecycle. This includes:
     - **Adaptive Control**: Dynamic adjustments to lighting/HVAC systems based on **occupancy and environmental data** (BOREALIS).
     - **Predictive Maintenance**: Early detection of **anomalies** in building systems via **anomaly detection algorithms** integrated into the DT.
  4. **Refurbishment/Adaptation**: The DT supports **retrofitting and renovation** by allowing **Facility Managers** to simulate and validate changes (e.g., adding new sensors, reconfiguring control logic) before physical implementation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The fidelity and validity of the DT are evaluated using the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**, which measures the alignment between the digital twin and its physical counterpart. This metric assesses the **interoperability** and **architectural soundness** of the DT system. While the document does not detail other validation mechanisms (e.g., automated data verification, sensor calibration), the ODTE metric provides a quantitative measure of the DT's accuracy and synchronization. The framework also emphasizes **semantic coherence** and **real-time data exchange** to maintain fidelity throughout the building lifecycle."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and the digital twin (DT) is facilitated by **Eclipse Hono** for IoT connectivity and **Eclipse Ditto** for digital twin management, enabling **real-time data exchange** via bidirectional communication protocols. While specific protocols like MQTT or HTTP/REST APIs are not explicitly mentioned, the framework leverages **standardized middleware** to ensure seamless integration. The connection supports **live synchronization** between physical assets and the DT, though details like hybrid cloud-edge deployment or service mesh architectures are not provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT framework adheres to **BIM as the semantic backbone** for building information, though specific standards like IFC or bSDD are not explicitly detailed. The system leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for real-time data exchange, which are open-source frameworks aligned with industry standards. While the document highlights the **lack of standardized integration mechanisms** as a challenge, it does not specify adherence to standards like MQTT, OMG's MDA, or ISO/IEC 27001. The framework emphasizes **interoperability** and **modularity** to support future standardization efforts."
]
